content.situation: You were part of a group project where team members were losing focus. You took the initiative to re-engage everyone.
content.speakerText: How did you get the team back on track?
content.speakerTextCn: 你是怎么让团队重新聚焦的？
content.responseTemplate: I noticed people were losing momentum, so I ___ and ___.
content.hint: Think about how you restarted communication and reminded the team of their purpose.
content.knowledgePoint: 团队激励与重新聚焦的表达策略

content.situation: During a team meeting, a colleague suggested an approach you thought wouldn’t work. You wanted to offer an alternative without causing conflict.
content.speakerText: What did you say when you disagreed with your teammate’s idea?
content.speakerTextCn: 当你不认同队友的想法时，你是怎么说的？
content.responseTemplate: Instead of saying it wouldn’t work, I asked, '___?' and shared a simpler version.

blanks.answer: What if we tried this instead
options: What if we tried this instead
I already did this last year
Why don’t you just listen to me

content.hint: Focus on collaborative language that invites input, not confrontation.
content.knowledgePoint: 建设性表达分歧的句型

content.situation: You noticed a teammate was struggling but didn’t speak up. You wanted to support them without making them feel singled out.
content.speakerText: How did you help them without putting them on the spot?
content.speakerTextCn: 你如何在不让他们难堪的情况下提供帮助？
content.responseTemplate: I ___ during a group chat and offered to ___ with them later.

options: told everyone they were falling behind
asked the leader to reassign them
made a joke about their work

content.hint: Use subtle, inclusive actions that offer support privately.
content.knowledgePoint: 隐性支持与团队关怀的表达方式
